name: Ganesha's Tusk
description: Ganesha's tusk is a significant symbol in Hindu mythology, representing sacrifice, wisdom, and the overcoming of obstacles. According to legend, Ganesha broke off one of his tusks to use it as a pen to write the Mahabharata as dictated by the sage Vyasa. This act of self-sacrifice demonstrates Ganesha's dedication to knowledge and the preservation of wisdom. The broken tusk also symbolizes the acceptance of imperfection and the ability to find strength in vulnerability. Some interpretations suggest that the unbroken tusk represents the spiritual realm, while the broken tusk represents the material world, signifying Ganesha's ability to bridge the gap between the two. Another account suggests Ganesha lost his tusk in a battle with Parashurama. Ganesha allowed Parashurama to strike him and break his tusk rather than retaliate, thus honoring his mother, Parvati, who had forbidden Parashurama from entering their home. The tusk is often depicted in Ganesha's iconography, serving as a reminder of his wisdom, selflessness, and the importance of perseverance in the face of adversity. It is a powerful symbol of his ability to remove obstacles and guide devotees towards enlightenment. The tusk is believed to hold immense power, capable of bestowing wisdom, removing obstacles, and granting success in endeavors. Its presence in temples and depictions of Ganesha serves as a constant reminder of these qualities.
subtitle: Symbol of sacrifice and wisdom.
origin: Broke off during Mahabharata writing or battle with Parashurama
currentLocation: Symbolic representation in temples and art

keyMyths.title: Writing the Mahabharata
keyMyths.description: Ganesha broke his tusk to use as a pen to write the Mahabharata as dictated by Vyasa, demonstrating his dedication to knowledge.

keyMyths.title: Battle with Parashurama
keyMyths.description: Ganesha allowed Parashurama to break his tusk rather than disobey his mother, showcasing his respect and devotion.
